title: Peptides. Fantastic business update with Kelly Parker, NP and Owner of Functional Health Warriors
description:
On this episode of the Chardon BIZ Podcast, Dave sits down with Kelly Parker from Functional Health Warriors for a deeper conversation around peptides—and what they actually mean for real people trying to take control of their health.

This isn’t a surface-level conversation or a trend-driven take. Most people are stuck in a system where they’re told to “wait and see,” manage symptoms, or guess their way through supplements and recovery. The result? Frustration, lost momentum, and a body that never quite performs the way it should.

Kelly breaks down:

• What peptides are in simple terms—and how they work in the body

• Where they fit (and don’t fit) in a complete health strategy

• The difference between chasing quick fixes vs. building a system that actually moves the needle

• Who should consider peptides—and who shouldn’t

• How peptides connect to bigger drivers like sleep, recovery, inflammation, and metabolic health

This episode ties directly into a bigger idea:

If you’re only looking at one piece of the puzzle—whether it’s training, nutrition, or even peptides—you’re missing the point. Real progress comes from coordination. That’s where the conversation shifts into how performance therapy, functional medicine, and accountability all work together to help people not just feel better—but actually perform at a higher level in their life.

If you’ve been told “everything looks normal” but you still don’t feel like yourself…This episode gives you a clearer path forward.

title: Hoss Boss Auto Detail
description: In this episode of the Chardon BIZ Podcast, host Dave Nelson sits down with Jimmy Borsi, owner of Hoss Boss Auto Detail, to talk about what it takes to build a premium service business on standards, consistency, and pride in the work. Jimmy shares the story behind Hoss Boss and how the company has earned a reputation for delivering the “new car” feel through high-level interior and exterior detailing, plus specialized services like paint correction and ceramic coating designed to protect and preserve a vehicle in the long term. This conversation is packed with real lessons on craftsmanship, customer trust, and what it looks like to keep raising the bar as a business grows.

title: Wyatt Works
description: In this episode of the Chardon BIZ Podcast, Dave Nelson sits down with Luke Wyatt of WyattWorks, a family-owned Cleveland home services company serving homeowners since 1980. WyattWorks helps people when home problems can’t wait—handling plumbing repairs, drain and sewer issues, and other essential home repair services with a focus on fast response and dependable results. Luke shares how they’ve built trust through consistent standards, clear communication, and doing the job right when it matters most.

title: Iron Castle Construction
description:
In this episode of Chardon BIZ, Dave Nelson sits down with Ken Schubert, veteran and owner of Iron Castle Construction Company, a family‑ and veteran‑owned business in Chardon dedicated to small and medium‑scale home renovations—from kitchens and bathrooms to hardwood floors and decks. Ken draws on over 25 years of construction and carpentry experience, sharing how his time leading the Army’s “Iron Castle” Engineer Brigade inspired not just the name of his company, but also its commitment to quality, trust, and communication, rooted in service and reliability.

Throughout the conversation, Ken opens up about what it takes to balance craftsmanship with responsiveness—how thoughtful attention to every detail, a guarantee of workmanship, and honest communication become the foundation of residential transformation and client trust. He invites listeners into stories of turning sagging, overlooked porches into welcoming entryways and customizing built‑ins with care—all while honoring values forged in leadership and integrity

title: Big Creek Clay
description:
In this episode of Chardon BIZ, host Dave Nelson sits down with Abbey Blake, from Big Creek Clay, a local clay studio that has become both a creative outlet and a community gathering place in Chardon. The conversation dives into the studio’s origin story—how a passion for pottery evolved into a business model centered on accessibility, creativity, and connection. From offering pottery classes and workshops to supplying materials for both beginners and advanced artists, Big Creek Clay shows how art can be a bridge between individual expression and community spirit.

Listeners will hear how the studio’s founders balance artistry with entrepreneurship, navigating challenges while staying rooted in their mission to foster curiosity and joy in hands-on learning. They share stories of the people who come through their doors—some discovering pottery for the first time, others returning to a long-forgotten passion—and how those experiences ripple outward into the larger Chardon community.

At its heart, this episode reflects how local businesses like Big Creek Clay embody presence and resilience. It’s about more than clay—it’s about shaping spaces where people feel welcomed, supported, and inspired. For business owners, it’s also a reminder that leading with authenticity and creativity can spark growth that extends well beyond the walls of a studio.

title: Ohio Ordinance Works
description:
In this episode, Dave Nelson dives into the story of Robert Landies and Ohio Ordnance Works, a unique and highly specialized local business that has made a name for itself in both the defense manufacturing industry and the Northeast Ohio community. The conversation explores the company’s deep-rooted history, from its origins producing military-grade firearms to its evolution into a cutting-edge operation known for innovation, quality, and craftsmanship. Listeners gain insight into the passion and precision behind the scenes and the challenges and rewards of operating a high-stakes business in a small-town setting.

More than just a manufacturing company, Ohio Ordnance Works stands as a proud example of how a locally owned business can thrive while staying true to its core values. The episode highlights their commitment to American manufacturing, workforce development, and local economic impact—demonstrating that excellence and community investment can go hand in hand. Whether you're into engineering, entrepreneurship, or just love hearing about standout local businesses, this episode offers a fascinating look at what it means to build something meaningful from the ground up.

title: The Hambone
description:
In this episode, Dave sits down with Nick Raischel, owner of The Hambone, a beloved neighborhood restaurant known for its great food, strong community roots, and welcoming atmosphere. Nick opens up about the journey of building The Hambone from the ground up—sharing the highs, lows, and everything in between that comes with running a small business in today’s world. From lessons learned through sports and life challenges to finding purpose in serving others, Nick brings humor, grit, and a deep sense of authenticity to the conversation.

Listeners will get a behind-the-scenes look at what it takes to run a community-centered restaurant and how Nick’s commitment to connection and consistency has made The Hambone more than just a place to eat. His passion for supporting local, creating meaningful relationships, and staying true to his values offers inspiration for small business owners and community members alike. Whether you’re in the restaurant world or love a good local success story, this episode delivers insight, laughs, and a whole lot of heart.

title: Functional Health Warriors
description:
Host: Dave Nelson – Owner of Chardon Performance Therapy and host of the Chardon BIZ Podcast, where local business leaders share their expertise, challenges, and successes.

Guest: Kelly Parker – Owner of Functional Health Warriors, a certified nurse practitioner with 16+ years of healthcare experience. Kelly is passionate about empowering individuals to take control of their health, drawing from both her professional background and personal journey overcoming health challenges.

Episode Focus:

In this episode, Dave sits down with Kelly to dive into the Six Pillars of Health—stress, sleep, nutrition & hydration, movement, community, and supplementation. Kelly shares how she helps clients achieve sustainable well-being, the challenges of navigating modern healthcare, and her own personal story of resilience that fuels her mission.

Expect an insightful conversation packed with practical tips on optimizing health, breaking through common wellness roadblocks, and taking a proactive approach to feeling your best.

Key Discussion Points:

*Kelly’s journey from traditional healthcare to a holistic approach

*The Functional Health Warriors philosophy and the Six Pillars of Health

*How small lifestyle changes can lead to significant long-term health benefits

*Common health misconceptions and practical strategies to overcome them

*The importance of a personalized approach to well-being

This episode is perfect for anyone looking to take charge of their health, prevent burnout, and build lasting wellness habits—whether you’re a busy professional, an athlete, or just trying to feel your best every day. Tune in now and take the first step toward better health! If this episode resonated with you, visit functionalhealthwarriors.org to learn more and start your wellness journey today!

description:

title: Maple Health Direct Primary Care
description: Simplifying healthcare, one conversation at a time! Tune in as Dave Nelson, owner of Chardon Performance Therapy and host of the ChardonBiz Podcast, sits down with Dr. Richard Berry from Maple Health DPC to learn more about their no-fuss, direct care approach that is changing the game of healthcare. Listen in as Dave and Dr. Berry dive into what is behind Maple Health DPC and how they offer stress-free access and availability of care to their patients.

title: Ubuntu Wellness- Update
description:
In this episode of the Chardon Biz Podcast, Dave talks to Jen and Erin about what's been going on with Ubuntu Wellness since our first podcast was recorded. In this update episode, we’re bringing you exciting updates from Ubuntu Wellness!

We’ll discuss some of the latest developments in health and fitness, including new programs, client success stories, and upcoming events. You’ll hear about innovative classes designed to boost mental and physical well-being, updates on partnerships with local health professionals, and how we’re staying at the forefront of personalized wellness solutions. Plus, get a sneak peek into their expanded services and community initiatives.

title: Fairmount Center for the Arts
description:
In this episode, Dave talks with Elizabeth Bolander, the Executive Director of Fairmount Center for the Arts. Listen in as they discuss the exciting news of the local art center opening a brand-new location in the heart of our Chardon community. Dave chats with Elizabeth about what this expansion means for the neighborhood, both culturally and creatively. From Farimount's mission to foster a local love for art to offering accessible art classes and workshops, this new location aims to be a vibrant hub for all art lovers. 

For more information visit https://www.fairmountcenter.org/.

title: K9 Heroes 4 Heroes
description:
Holy smokes! Looking up Dedication and Drive online, you will see Joe Gionti’s name right there. We are excited for you to listen to how Joe has created a phenomenal Non-Profit focused on delivering trained service-dogs to service men and women, and he is making this happen while maintaining a full-time job.

The collaborations surrounding the massive efforts Joe is putting forth are impressive and it is important to shout out these incredible organizations: Ridgeside K9 training and boarding, Mutts with a Mission, Forgotten Coast, Project K9 Hero, Sub Zero Mission, Lions not Sheep, and SAGE Integration..

Visit K9 Heroes 4 Heroes website to learn more about becoming a part of Joe's mission.

title: Holstein Homes
description: This week on the Chardon BIZ podcast, join your host Dave Nelson, owner of Chardon Performance Therapy, as he sits down to explore a local family-owned real estate and investment business that uniquely combines Holstein cows, on-call hot water tank repairs, and marriage advice in their many facets of service. Discover how these elements together form a remarkable local business worth exploring further. Tune in to hear more about this local business!

title: Mother Rising
description: This week on the Chardon BIZ podcast, join your host Dave Nelson, owner of Chardon Performance Therapy, as he sits down with Shauna Rich, Co-Founder of Mother Rising, located on the Chardon Square above Remax. Listen in to hear Shauna tell us about the passion behind Mother Rising and how she finds support and accountability in the community around her. Shauna tells us how she is taking steps towards better mental health, supporting maternal well-being, and embracing accountability in her own life despite being a professional. Tune in to hear Mother Rising's story.

title: NEO ELECTRIC
description: This week on the Chardon BIZ podcast, join your host Dave Nelson, owner of Chardon Performance Therapy, as he sits down with Steve Hughes, Co-Owner of NEO Electric, located next door to King Kone. Discover how NEO Electric brings unparalleled customer service and expertise in electrical and lighting, this business isn't just a service provider; it's a cornerstone of the community. Here, neighbors aren't just clients; they're treated like extended family members, receiving personalized attention and top-notch solutions for all their electrical needs. Tune in to hear Steve's story!

title: Chardon Area Chamber of Commerce Q4 2023 Q1 2024
description:
How much do you know about what's going on in your community? What do you know about the local businesses, events, and the people that hold it all together? This week your host Dave Nelson owner of Chardon Performance Therapy sits down with Melisssa Ricco the Executive Director of the Chardon Chamber of Commerce. Take a listen as they dive into all the amazing things the businesses in our area are doing to not only improve our community but bring it closer together. Mellissa and the Chardon Area Chamber of Commerce have done a wonderful job of promoting businesses like:

Cremona

The People's Pantry

The Ox Shoppe/ Broken Yolk Ranch

Evolved Transporters

Pop Shelf

Snap Fitness

Y.E.S. Fitness & Wellness

NAMI Geauga

Supporting your community businesses is what keeps them flourishing, Tune in to see how you can show up for the businesses that will always show up for you! #chardonohio #chardonbiz #chardonperformancetherapy #chardonpt #shoplocal #communitysupport #Chardonchamber

title: Boost Mode (Tina Butt)
description: This week on the Chardon BIZ podcast, join your host Dave Nelson, owner of Chardon Performance Therapy, as he sits down with Tina Butt, CEO and Owner of Boost Mode Vitadrip Spa located beneath Sweets on the Square. Discover how Boost Mode goes beyond rehydration, offering evidence-based IV hydration for overall health and wellness. Tina shares insights into the health challenges her clients face and the personalized onboarding process. Tune in to hear Tina's engaging approach as she encourages you to embrace a challenge for improved self-control in your health journey. Connect with her for a conversation or drop by – you won't be disappointed!

title: Iron Athlete (Bryan Doberdruk)
description: Meet Bryan Doberdruk on the Chardon BIZ podcast, where his gravitational, loud personality shines through in the first 5 minutes. Discover his inspiring journey, rooted in humble achievements and a passion for youth athletics. Uncover the 'secret sauce' of his success, characterized by positive grit, as he recounts stories from Clinton Massie to founding Strength Lab and Iron Athlete. Special mention to Theresa Straight, collaborator on Iron Individual, contributing to the outstanding culture they're building in Painesville. Chardon Performance Therapy is excited for future collaborations with Bryan, Theresa, and Iron Athlete, shaping one of the area's best cultures.

title: Your CBD Store Chardon (Lauren & Aiden)
description: What’s your familiarity with CBD? Whether you have a positive, negative, or uncertain opinion about it, Your CBD Store Chardon offers a fresh perspective. In this episode, your host Dave Nelson, owner of Chardon Performance Therapy, sits down with Lauren Luma, one of the founders of Your CBD Store Chardon, and her son, Aiden. Lauren candidly discusses her personal experience with CBD and how it can benefit you. She also shares her journey to discovering Your CBD Store. It’s worth noting that Your CBD Store Chardon exclusively uses Sunmed products, rigorously tested and ethically sourced to ensure safety and quality for customers. Tune in to explore how CBD might improve your well-being or that of someone you know.

title: Chardon Chamber of Commerce - Mellissa Ricco
description:
How much do you know about what's going on in your community? The businesses, events, and the people that hold it all together? This week your host Dave Nelson owner of Chardon Performance Therapy sits down with Melissa Ricco the Executive Director of the Chardon Chamber of Commerce. Take a listen as they dive into all the amazing things the businesses in our area are doing to not only improve our community but bring it closer together. Mellissa and the Chardon Area Chamber of Commerce have done a wonderful job of promoting businesses like:

Cometic Gasket, Inc.

Chardon Lakes Golf Course

Brown Barn Tavern

ISSQUARED Inc

Louie's Laundromat 

Carriage Realty

Eleventhree Brewing

Basil Place

Chardon Local Schools

Geauga Growth Partnership, Inc.

 Patterson Fruit Farm

D&S Automotive Collision and Restyling

The Lodge at Bass Lake

 Supporting your community businesses is what keeps them flourishing, Tune in to see how you can show up for the businesses that will always show up for you!

title: ElevenThree, Chelsea & Dave Wickman
description: Did you know Chardon OHIO has its very own Brewery? Are you curious about who owns it and what they want to share with our community? This week your Host, Dave Nelson with the Chardon BIZ podcast and owner of Chardon Performance Therapy dives into Chelsea & Dave Wickmans story of how they started Eleventhree brewery! A new business does not just happen on its own and you can hear from Dave and Chelsea how collaborations from other businesses has helped them get started. Shout out to Sugar Bush, Wilmington North Carolina Brewery, Crooked Pecker,  and Chardon Square. We had so much fun recording this story for you all to listen to. Please like and share to show support for #shoplocalchardon A big congratulations to Dave and Chelsea on their huge success with their open house that was held on 8/30/23.

title: The Baby Fairy Shoppe, Robin Barber.
description: This week your Host, Dave Nelson with the Chardon BIZ podcast and owner of Chardon Performance Therapy, sits down with Robin Barber, The founder and owner of The Baby Fairy Shoppe. Robin also spends a lot of time networking utilizing grassroots efforts with organizations like the Chardon Chamber of Commerce. The Baby Fairy Shoppe is on a mission to bring expecting moms a little bit of extra joy on their journey. They started by sending packages through the mail to one of their expecting friends from the “Baby Fairy”.  Allowing friends and family to anonymously send gifts throughout pregnancy to give mom something extra to look forward to you Choose items from their collections, they ship them to your expecting mom separately throughout the pregnancy! Each package is delivered with a special note enclosed, bringing an air of mystery and excitement, at the end they’ll send a card revealing you as the baby fairy! Getting ready to be a mom can be difficult, whether its your first time or you’ve been through it before, getting a little extra love and pampering throughout can make a world of difference. Thanks to The Baby Fairy Shoppe you can send a little extra love to the soon to be momma in your life!

title: Broken Yoke Ranch, Dawn and Darrell Flemming-Kendall.
description: Broken Yoke Ranch, owned and operated by Darrell and Dawn Fleming-Kendall, is located right in Thompson Ohio. They provide fresh quality produce as well as different meats and poultry at great prices, that can all be found in their farm store called the Oxe Shoppe. This is all awesome but that’s not it, what they really excel at is providing hope and training to people with barriers to employment. This leads to greater production on local farms and provides meaningful jobs that teach a certain skill set to those who may need the extra leg up. The Broken Ranch is a non-profit organization currently collaborating with Brandon Chrostowski from the restaurant Edwin’s to host the first fundraiser. The Public event is coming up on August 17th from 6-8pm at The Western Reserve Land Conservancy and will be a local dinner and wine pairing with ALL of the food and drinks being sourced locally! Tickets will be $200 and will go towards furthering the substantial and incredible dreams at the Broken Yoke Ranch!

title: Delta Sales Associates Inc.
description: Les Murphy, with Delta Sales Associates Inc. is not just a business owner helping his industry in a huge way, he is also an incredible community member with his involvement in the Chardon Chamber, his interest in being a solid father, husband, and foster parent for 4 legged creatures as well and children. Delta Sales is a leader in on-site calibration services. They specialize in instrumentation to measure coating thicknesses. They also calibrate the test equipment testing methods associated with the application and qualification of the coatings. In addition, they offer repair service, rental and new equipment sales.

title: The Learning Cafe (TLC)
description:
This week, Dave talks with Tania Bertolone, Founder and Director of The Learning Café (TLC), a homeschool enrichment community located in the heart of Chardon. Hear Tania converse about the role TLC plays in our community: adding freedom, flexibility, and choice to the families of northeast Ohio. Their mission is to "create a nurturing and loving community that prioritizes individual wellbeing and encourages and inspires students to engage in meaningful joyful learning giving them the tools to become happy healthy successful adults."

Check out their website to learn more!

https://www.thelearningcafecommunity.com/

title: Salty REPOST
description:
ALLERGY SEASON IS UPON US!!!! In honor of spring making its way into Chardon, we decided to have a repost featuring SALTY! Owners Candice and Brandon Zinner created Salty as a place for anyone to relax, recharge, and rejuvenate after experiencing the benefits of integrating halotherapy and natural remedies into their own self-care. Candice, specifically, discovered it helped with her allergies most. In this fast-paced, always-on-the-go society, it’s important to slow down and recharge. So, take a few moments for yourself. Breathe in and relax.

If you or someone you know has a local Chardon business who is involved in our community and would like to hear on our podcast, send us an email at dave@chardonpt.com

title: King Kong REPOST
description: The "scoop" on a local favorite...KING KONE!!!!

title: The Collective Rose & Blue Lake Cleaning
description:
Learn how this married couple compliment their businesses and dreams, along with raising their 1 year old handsome little dude. Did you know Erica finds her success with the incredible amount of collaborations she puts together with businsnesses and also her fashion sense. Nate, also a Geauga Co Sheriff (Thank you for your service) gives other first responders opportunities to work with his business.

https://www.collectiverose.com/

https://www.bluelakecleaningllc.com/

title: Chardon Restaurant Week 2023
description:
A behind the scenes look into the 3rd annual Restaraunt week in Chardon. Have you wondered how it got started, who is involved, or how it impacts our community in a great way?

Have a listen to this weeks podcast episode highlighting the amazing people behind Chardon Restaraunt week. Find out more information by going to https://www.chardonrestaurantweek.com 

Thank you to the following great people for helping to make this event happen!

Joyce Perrico, organizer of Chardon’s Restaurant week

Jim Boardwine, Thrive Theatre Director

Melissa Ricco, Chardon Area Chamber of Commerce Director

title: Chardon Square Association Q1 2023 Update
description: This week, Dave talks with Sylvia McGee, President of the Chardon Square Association regarding events in the upcoming months. Be sure to mark your calendars for these! Don't forget to subscribe to the Chardon Square Association Newsletter for all events at https://chardonsquareassociation.org/

title: Chardon Chamber of Commerce 2023 Q1 Update
description:
Dave collaborate with Melissa Ricco, Executive Director of the Chardon Area Chamber of Commerce. Engaging in conversation about new Ribbon Cutting Ceremonies and "all things local" going on in our community during the first quarter of 2023.

Hope to see you at Alvords Insurance Ribbon cutting THIS THURSDAY at 5!

Are you a business owner in Chardon, or care about Thinking Local, stay up to date with our quarterly updates on events you will want to hear about. This link will take you to the Chamber Website Events tab where you can find out more information and register.

#ThinkLocal #ShopLocal #SupportLocal #ChardonOhio

https://business.chardonchamber.com/eventcalendar

title: Square Up Barbershop Part 2
description: Happy Monday! Part 2 of the Square Up Barbershop podcast episode with Chad Horvath. Give a listen to hear Chad's amazing backstory. Everyone loves a true underdog story and Chad's is as inspirational as it gets!

title: Square Up Barbershop Part 1
description: This week your host, Dave Nelson, talks with Chad Horvath, Owner of Square Up Barbershop located right on our Chardon Square. Featured in The News Herald, "Unlike the shops of old, Square Up reflects a modern industrial look featuring warm woods, strong metals and other trimmings." You can hear the passion behind Chad's story. Dave loves his haircuts so much, he has vowed to be a lifelong customer!

title: Geauga Growth Partnership (GGP)
description:
This week, your host Dave Nelson talks with Kimm Leininger, Present & CEO of Geauga Growth Partnership. GGP strives to create Leading Business Growth in Geauga County.

Geauga Growth Partnership’s mission is to enhance the economic climate in Geauga County by attracting new business, supporting existing businesses, and preparing the workforce of the future.

title: G.A.R. Horizons
description:
This week, Dave chats with Catherine Wolcott, founder and owner of G.A.R. Horizons Freezer Meats. G.A.R. Horizons has been raising quality meats since 2009. First generation, no Farmers in the family, starting from scratch! Animals are raised with love and care, they are grown naturally, hormone free, steroid free and GMO free. Animals feed is made at the farm by Steve with his John Deere tractor and grinder mixer. Their newly expanded store includes a variety of local products including milk, and pierogies (yes you read that right!).

G.A.R. Horizons is a member of the Geauga County Farm Bureau, Geauga County 4-H, Chardon Square Association, Chardon Chamber of Commerce. We are so proud to have them in our community! Be sure to swing by and check out their awesome store!

https://www.garhorizons.com

title: Chardon Polka Band Part 2
description:
Dave interviews Jake Kouwe, founder of Chardon Polka Band and new owner of Chip's Clubhouse. Hear Jake's inspiring story as he discusses the early years of the band and the inspiration behind his early love for music. This two part episode is perfect entertainment for the family roadtrip this holiday season. Don't forget to check out Chardon Polka Band's music and upcoming shows! Thanks Jake! As Dave says, you are a "cool dude".

This podcast is available on Spotify, Apple Podcast and our website https://www.chardonphysicaltherapy.com/podcast

Thanks so much for listening to Chardon Biz

description:
Dave interviews Jake Kouwe, founder of Chardon Polka Band and new owner of Chip's Clubhouse. Hear Jake's inspiring story as he discusses the early years of the band and the inspiration behind his early love for music. title: Dottie’s Flower Farm AND Sweets on the Square
description:
Your host of Chardon BIZ interviewed Catie Traffis, owner of Dottie’s Flower Farm, Sweets on the Square, a wife, mother of 3 great kids, member of Chardon Square Association and much more. Here a fabulous story which was recorded on Catie’s farm in Chardon. Get more information on here farm and offerings here https://www.dottiesflowerfarm.com

PS, did you know they have donated all the candy for the 2022 Halloween on the Square!!!!

Thank you Catie and Jeff Traffis for all you do for the Chardon Community.

title: Chamber of Commerce Q4 Updates 2022
description:
Q4 update with Melissa Ricco, Executive Director of the Chardon Area Chamber of Commerce and Heather Means Vice Mayor and member of the Economic Development team

Discussion: Events that took place in October, ribbon cuttings coming up in November, current business development in Chardon, vision and current implementation of resources the Chamber has available for businesses in Chardon.

Additional business discussed:

title: Square Association Q4 Update 2022
description:
Q4 Chardon Square Association update with Sylvia McGee, President and Ed Slusarski Vice President

Discussion: What and who is the Square Association. Upcoming events for Halloween and Christmas. Shout out to Elliot Miller @scuze.me.i.arted, artist who painted the mural at 106 Water Street and the Giving Garden.

Dates of note (Read more on Chardon Square website)

  • Oct 31 from 7:30-8 Halloween on the Square
  • Dec 1 from 6:30-8 Christmas Lighting of the Square
  • Dec 10 from 10-5 Home for the Holidays

Additional business discussed: 

  • Sweets on the Square donation of candy for Halloween on the Square
  • Scarecrow decorating contest sponsored by Mapledale Farms

Visit the Chardon Square website https://chardonsquareassociation.org for more information. Look forward to Q1 update for 2023 soon!!

title: Mapledale Farm
description: This week your host, Dave Nelson, CEO of Chardon Physical Therapy, sits down with Dave Johnson, President of Mapledale Landscaping!What a pleasure it was to sit with Dave and discuss the history of Mapledale, his amazing team who operates it and the passion involved in running a local business.If you would like to leave feedback or have any comments, please reach out to dave@chardonpt.com

title: #ChardonBiz Summer Update!
description:
Check out this week’s mini-episode to discover some exciting updates for our podcast and the Chardon Physical Therapy practice!

Although we are in the midst of a short hiatus through August, we can’t wait to catch up with you in September!

Please rate us on Apple Podcasts or Spotify! If you would like to nominate your favorite business for a featured episode, please reach out to team@chardonpt.com

title: Qualified Wellness Solutions
description:
Combatting mental burnout and the physical symptoms that typically accompany it can be hard, but it doesn’t have to be…

This week your host, Dave Nelson, chats with Tina Butt of Qualified Wellness Solutions about the importance of becoming your own advocate!

Please rate us on Apple Podcasts or Spotify! If you would like to leave feedback, please reach out to team@chardonpt.com

title: Chardon Smile Center on the Square
description:
We are all smiles about this next episode!

Your host, Dave Nelson, met up with Dr. Brian from Chardon Smile Center. Their chat on the Square is full of laughs as they discuss Brian’s career and his passion for his patients.

Please rate us on Apple Podcasts or Spotify! If you would like to leave feedback, please reach out to team@chardonpt.com

title: The Inside Scoop on KingKone
description:
We’re kicking off this week with a legendairy interview! Your host, Dave Nelson, gets the inside scoop on one of Chardon’s favorite heart-warming duos!

Listen this week for the play-by-play with Mitch and Gail of KingKone!

Please rate us on Apple Podcasts or Spotify! If you would like to leave feedback, please reach out to team@chardonpt.com

title: A BONUS Interview with Bill Cox
description:
This week on Chardon Biz, your host Dave Nelson is delivering a special BONUS episode with Bill Cox! (If you haven’t had the chance, make sure you listen to Part 1, Part 2, and Part 3!)

In this final episode, Bill tells us what’s next, and what’s in store for his future (spoiler, his future may be outside of Chardon!)

On behalf of all of us at Chardon Biz, we are so grateful for the time and the trust Bill has in us to share his story with all of you. We hope you enjoy our final episode with the man who founded Comic Art Fans and originally owned Chip’s Clubhouse.

Please rate us on Apple Podcasts or Spotify! If you would like to leave feedback, please reach out to team@chardonpt.com

title: An interview with Bill Cox – Part 3
description:
In this week’s episode of Chardon Biz, your host Dave Nelson continues to uncover the story of Bill Cox. (Check out Part 1 and Part 2 here!)

In episode three, Bill tells us how he navigated the complicated aspects of the pandemic and how he expanded CAF and created Comic Art Live.

Please rate us on Apple Podcasts or Spotify! If you would like to leave feedback, please reach out to team@chardonpt.com

title: An interview with Bill Cox – Part 2
description:
In this week’s episode of Chardon Biz, your host Dave Nelson continues to uncover the story of Bill Cox. (Check out Part 1 here!)

In episode two, Bill tells us how he broke the mold of his “mechanic” mindset, that he describes in last week’s episode.

Stay tuned for next week’s concluding episode in our 3-part series!

Please rate us on Apple Podcasts or Spotify! If you would like to leave feedback, please reach out to team@chardonpt.com

title: An interview with Bill Cox – Part 1
description:
Chardon Biz is back with a special episode featuring the founder of Comic Art Fans and Chip’s Clubhouse.

This is the only place you will get to hear the incredible story of Bill Cox. Over the next three weeks, your host, Dave Nelson, will uncover his story in 3 episodes – so stay tuned!

Please rate us on Apple Podcasts or Spotify! If you would like to leave feedback, please reach out to team@chardonpt.com

title: Wednesdays Private Home Cleaning LLC
description:
After our break, we could not be more excited to introduce you to this week’s guest.

This week your host, Dave Nelson, CEO of Chardon Physical Therapy, sits down with Wednesday Nelson, owner of Wednesdays Private Home Cleaning.

We chat about her debut in Chardon and discuss how opening your own business can grant you so many freedoms!

Please rate us on Apple Podcasts or Spotify! If you would like to leave feedback, please reach out to team@chardonpt.com

title: Maggie’s Doughnuts
description:
This week your host, Dave Nelson, CEO of Chardon Physical Therapy, sits down with Alissa Hogue, owner of Maggie’s Doughnuts.

Alissa’s journey as an entrepreneur is quite impressive – at only 18 she now owns her own franchise of Maggie’s. We loved hearing her story and can’t wait to see what’s in store for her in the future!

Please rate us on Apple Podcasts or Spotify! If you would like to leave feedback, please reach out to team@chardonpt.com

title: Denise – D is for Delicious
description:
This week your host, Dave Nelson, CEO of Chardon Physical Therapy, sits down with Denise, owner of D is for Delicious!

It was such a pleasure to visit Denise’s shop and chat about her passion for cookies. We hope you enjoy this week’s episode and that it inspires you to check out her online store for a sweet treat!

If you would like to leave feedback or have any comments, please reach out to dave@chardonpt.com

title: Ben Sage – Ransom Sage Farm
description:
This week your host, Dave Nelson, CEO of Chardon Physical Therapy, sits down with Ben, owner of Ransom Sage Farm!

Ben treats Dave to a tour of the farm, a few amazing ciders that they produce on the property, tales of the farm’s history, and more!

If you would like to leave feedback or have any comments, please reach out to dave@chardonpt.com .

title: Scott – Chardon ATA Martial Arts
description:
This week your host, Dave Nelson, CEO of Chardon Physical Therapy, sits down with Scott, owner of Chardon ATA Martial Arts!

What a pleasure it was to sit with Scott and discuss his business, but also the inspiration and drive he hopes to pass on to his students.

If you would like to leave feedback or have any comments, please reach out to dave@chardonpt.com

title: Eric & Michelle – Basil Place
description:
This week your host, Dave Nelson, CEO of Chardon Physical Therapy, sits down with Michelle & Eric, owners of Basil Place! Basil Place is a farm and brand new wedding venue on a private, 100-acre estate.

I was so grateful to have the chance to chat with them about all the details that went into creating such a unique experience for their guests.

If you would like to leave feedback or have any comments, please reach out to dave@chardonpt.com

title: Michelle & Lori – Chardon Integrated Wellness Center
description:
This week your host, Dave Nelson, CEO of Chardon Physical Therapy, sits down with Michelle & Lori, owners of Chardon Integrated Wellness Center! It was an honor to chat with them about their dedication to their patients and community.

If you would like to leave feedback or have any comments, please reach out to dave@chardonpt.com

title: Freia Ramsey – Taproot Yoga
description:
This week your host, Dave Nelson, CEO of Chardon Physical Therapy, sits down with the owner of Taproot Yoga, Freia Ramsey! Freia is a dear friend of the podcast and we couldn’t be more thrilled to chat with her in this week’s episode.

If you would like to leave feedback or have any comments, please reach out to dave@chardonpt.com

title: Arthur Brite
description: To kick off the New Year, we have a very special guest this week: your host, Dave Nelson, CEO of Chardon Physical Therapy, sits down with Arthur Brite! Arthur’s story as a keynote speaker and author is definitely inspiring!

title: Sylvia McGee – Beans Coffee Shop & Bistro
description:
This week your host, Dave Nelson, CEO of Chardon Physical Therapy, sits down with the owner of Beans Coffee Shop & Bistro, Sylvia McGee! They chat about what it’s like to own a business on the square and how important it is to provide our community with a little piece of home.

If you would like to leave feedback or have any comments, please reach out to dave@chardonpt.com

title: Candice – Salty
description:
Your host, Dave Nelson, CEO of Chardon Physical Therapy, sits down with the owner of Salty, Candice Zinner! Check out their discussion about Chardon’s own Salt Spa.

If you would like to leave feedback or have any comments, please reach out to dave@chardonpt.com

title: Jen Emch & Erin Moore – Ubuntu Wellness
description:
Your host, Dave Nelson, CEO of Chardon Physical Therapy, sits down with a couple of very special guests, Jennifer Emch and Erin Moore. Jen & Erin are the owners of Ubuntu Wellness, which specializes in providing our community with holistic health services that enhance the mind, body, and spirit.

If you would like to leave feedback or have any comments, please reach out to dave@chardonpt.com

title: Dan Sammon- Everybodies Gym
description:
Your host, Dave Nelson, CEO of Chardon Physical Therapy presents a very special guest,Dan Sammon. Dan is the owner of Geauga County’s largest gym and also a part owner of Pioneer Waterland. Key takeaway from this podcast is that history will show if you put in hard work, you will succeed. AND WAIT!!! Did he mentioned the Hells Angels?

If you would like to leave feedback or have any comments, please reach out to dave@chardonpt.com

title: CJ Paterniti – D&S Automotive
description:
Your host, Dave Nelson, CEO of Chardon Physical Therapy presents a very special guest, CJ Paterniti. CJ is the owner of D&S automotive located in Chardon and Mentor. He is much more than a business owner so I recommend taking a listen to hear how his success in his business translates to current and future positive community involvement. A key take away as a listener was learning how to continually press forward when some folks try to knock you down by identifying what drives you.

If you would like to leave any comments, please email dave@chardonpt.com
